seoTitle: How to Create an Incoming Webhook URL in Slack
metaDescription: Generate a Slack incoming webhook URL through the developer dashboard so external scripts and tools can post messages to a channel.
intro: Connecting a third-party server or custom script to your Slack workspace requires a secure entry point. This guide covers creating an app, enabling Incoming Webhooks, and generating a URL you can use to send messages programmatically.

steps.heading: Create a new app
steps.body: Go to the Slack API dashboard and click 'Create New App' to start a fresh application.

steps.heading: Choose From Scratch
steps.body: Select 'From Scratch', name your app, and pick the workspace you want it installed in.

steps.heading: Enable Incoming Webhooks
steps.body: Scroll to Features, click Incoming Webhooks, and toggle the activation switch to On.

steps.heading: Add a webhook to your workspace
steps.body: Scroll down and click 'Add New Webhook to Workspace', then select a target channel and click Allow.

steps.heading: Copy and test the URL
steps.body: Copy the generated webhook URL, then run a sample curl command from a terminal to confirm messages post correctly.

faq.question: Is the webhook URL sensitive information?
faq.answer: Yes, treat it like a secret since anyone with the URL can post messages to your channel.

faq.question: Can one app have multiple webhook URLs?
faq.answer: Yes, you can add separate webhooks for different channels within the same app.

faq.question: What format does the webhook expect for messages?
faq.answer: Incoming webhooks accept a JSON payload, typically with a 'text' field for the message content.

faq.question: How do I revoke a webhook if it's compromised?
faq.answer: Go back to your app's Incoming Webhooks page and remove the specific webhook to disable it immediately.
